pr.title: Quick match of a Peach function
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched one Peach fighter function, func_8011B51C, by moving its body from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpeach.s into new C source/header files and wiring ftpeach.o into the build. The C implementation initializes several Fighter fields around offsets 0x222C-0x2248, calls func_80074A4C for article/slot-like indices 0,2,3,4, then branches on Fighter::x619_flag to choose different argument patterns for indices 1,5,6. Supporting header work split the Fighter filler enough to expose x618_flag and x619_flag and added x2244/x2248 fields; func_80074A4C's third parameter was corrected from u32 to s32 to allow -1 arguments.
what_changed: Removed the assembly implementation of func_8011B51C from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpeach.s, leaving the remaining Peach assembly including ftPeach_OnLoad and later labels in place.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftpeach.c with a C implementation of func_8011B51C(HSD_GObj* gobj).
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftpeach.h with includes for global.h, dolphin/types.h, HSD_GObj, Fighter, and a prototype for func_8011B51C.

Added a stub C definition of ftPeach_OnLoad(HSD_GObj* gobj) in ftpeach.c, while the diff excerpt still shows ftPeach_OnLoad assembly remaining in ftpeach.s; exact build/link implications are not explained in PR text.
Changed Fighter layout in src/melee/ft/fighter.h from one large filler up to 0x222C to exposed fields at x618_flag and x619_flag, then filler to x222C, and added x2244 and x2248 after x2240.
Changed include/functions.h prototype for func_80074A4C from third argument u32 to s32, matching the decompiled calls that pass -1.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftpeach.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk and to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before the remaining asm ftpeach.o.
smart_moves: Used a minimal struct-splitting approach in Fighter: only the offsets needed by the matched function were named, while the rest stayed as filler arrays to preserve layout.
Corrected func_80074A4C's third parameter to signed because the matched function passes both 0 and -1; this avoids misleading casts or unsigned constants in the C match.

Kept the implementation straightforward and close to the assembly: initialize fields in the same order, issue func_80074A4C calls in the same sequence, and use a simple switch on x619_flag for the two observed branches.
Integrated the newly matched C object without deleting the whole ftpeach assembly file, enabling partial decompilation of a single function while preserving unmatched Peach code.
decomp_lessons: When matching code that passes -1 to an existing unknown function, revisit shared prototypes; a signed parameter may be necessary for clean matching and better semantic accuracy.
Partial fighter decompilation can expose specific Fighter offsets as named fields while retaining filler for unknown regions, rather than waiting for a full struct map.
For character files with mixed matched and unmatched functions, add the new src object alongside the existing asm object and remove only the matched assembly function.
Branch patterns comparing a one-byte Fighter flag can often be expressed as a switch or if/default in C; here switch (ft->x619_flag) with case 1/default represented the assembly's cmpwi/beq flow.
naming_conventions: New Peach files follow the character naming pattern src/melee/ft/chara/ftpeach.c and src/melee/ft/chara/ftpeach.h.
The header guard used was _ftpeach_h_, matching a lowercase file-name-derived style present in the submitted code.
Unknown function and field names remained address/offset based: func_8011B51C, func_80074A4C, x618_flag, x619_flag, x222C, x2234, x2248.
Fighter filler fields were named data_filler_1 and data_filler_2 to preserve layout without claiming semantics.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The removed assembly stores gobj->user_data from offset 0x2C into r31; the C uses Fighter* ft = gobj->user_data.
The stores to 0x222C, 0x2234, 0x2240, 0x223C, 0x2238, 0x2244, and 0x2248 were represented as direct Fighter field assignments before any helper calls.
Repeated calls to func_80074A4C were reproduced with literal indices and signed third arguments: (0,0), (2,0), (3,-1), (4,0), then branch-specific calls for 1,5,6.
The assembly branch on byte offset 0x619 became switch (ft->x619_flag), with case 1 using third arguments -1,0,-1 and default using 0,-1,0.
The third argument type change to s32 in include/functions.h supports matching calls with li r5, -1 without unsigned-type friction.
searchable_terms: Quick match of a Peach function
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for whether ftPeach_OnLoad's empty C stub was retained, removed, or matched, since this PR excerpt shows both a C stub and remaining assembly label.
Search for later refinements to Fighter fields x618_flag and x619_flag to learn their semantic names.
Search for later updates to func_80074A4C naming and signature, especially whether its second argument represents an article/accessory slot or another fighter subsystem index.
Compare this match against adjacent Peach setup/onload functions to identify recurring initialization patterns for x222C through x2248.
